sourceMovie.title: Mr. Deeds
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Mr. Deeds works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pretty Woman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mr. Deeds clicked for you, Pretty Woman (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mr. Deeds?
faq.answer: Try Pretty Woman, The Kissing Booth 2, Breakfast at Tiffany's—each shares DNA with Mr. Deeds while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mr. Deeds?
faq.answer: Mr. Deeds (2002) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mr. Deeds worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
